name: Wisconsin Respiratory Therapist License
description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Wisconsin - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Wisconsin DSPS — Respiratory Care Practitioner
provider.parentOrganization: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)

address.description:
Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services
P.O. Box 8368
Madison, WI 53708-8368

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Wisconsin respiratory therapist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.
Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Wisconsin. To work in Wisconsin, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Wisconsin?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
